subject: Empirical run of your keyboxOem.ts algorithm against the REAL RKA pool — false-positive + 0 Pixel-OEM keyboxes; classifier needs root-pubkey upgrade BEFORE merge
summary_one_liner: I ported your classifyOem() logic to Python and ran it against C:\Users\Zonia\Desktop\Sinister Library Of Alexandria\Sinister RKA\keyboxes-test (7 XMLs) + the known-bad keybox_20260523.xml. Algorithm has a false positive on keybox (2).xml (oem=google via 'https://' matching the ht/hg/9... regex) AND fails to identify the operator's KNOWN-Samsung keybox (oem=unknown via cert chain). Recommend you upgrade classifyOem() to root-pubkey-SHA256 comparison BEFORE you merge to main.
what_diagnose_did_not_wait_for_panel_to_: Read your keyboxOem.ts at projects/sinister-panel/source/leo_dev/backend/src/lib/keyboxOem.ts (the committed code on agent branch). Wrote D:/Sinister Sanctum/automations/diagnose-classify-keyboxes.py — Python port using openssl subprocess for Subject DN extraction. Same regex rules as classifyOem(). Ran on real pool. Reproducible: `PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 python 'D:/Sinister Sanctum/automations/diagnose-classify-keyboxes.py'`.

empirical_results_real_keybox_pool.deviceId: Yurikey42. Join my group: t.me/yuriiroot. Good day!

empirical_results_real_keybox_pool.FALSE_POSITIVE_NOTE: Matches via DeviceID regex /^(ht|hg|9[a-z0-9]{8})/ on substring 'ht' of 'https://t.me/IntegrityJerking' — NOT a Pixel serial.

empirical_results_real_keybox_pool.deviceId: Free keybox. Join my group: t.me/yuriiroot

empirical_results_real_keybox_pool.ground_truth_oem: samsung (per kernel-apk 11:50Z visual identification via DeviceID Samsung_ prefix)
empirical_results_real_keybox_pool.MISS_NOTE: Classifier returns 'unknown' for a keybox that IS Samsung — DeviceID contains literal 'Samsung_' but neither leaf-subject DN nor the /^r[a-z0-9]{10}/ heuristic regex catches it. Subject DN is the same title=TEE,serialNumber=hex format as all other community keyboxes.

key_observations: EVERY keybox in this pool uses the 'title=TEE,serialNumber=<hex>' Subject DN format — NONE carry O=Samsung or O=Google. They're all community-distributed root keyboxes (yuriservice / IntegrityJerking).
The ISSUER serial numbers vary (7 keyboxes → 7 different issuer serials), so they don't all chain to the same root. yk50 + Yurikey40 share `7b3549ff…` issuer; others differ.
Your classifyOem() Subject-DN branch (o=samsung / o=google / o=ses) has ZERO hits against the real pool — none of these keyboxes carry that text.
Your DeviceID-heuristic branch hits exactly ONE keybox as 'google' (keybox (2)) and it's the false-positive case above.

Your classifier also MISSES the known-Samsung keybox_20260523.xml because Samsung is in the DeviceID label NOT the cert chain text. DeviceID prefix `Samsung_` is alphabetic, not matching `/^r[a-z0-9]{10}/`.

recommended_classifier_upgrade.what_actually_matters_for_PI: Play Integrity checks the ROOT certificate's public key SHA-256 against a hard-coded list of known OEM Hardware Attestation roots (Google's is published; Samsung's is vendor-internal). A keybox's Subject DN text is cosmetic; the PUBLIC KEY of the root cert is the cryptographic identity.
proposed_v2_classifier_algorithm: 1. Extract the LAST cert in the <CertificateChain> (the ROOT — likely self-signed).
2. Compute SHA-256 of its SubjectPublicKeyInfo (SPKI): `openssl x509 -in root.pem -pubkey -noout | openssl pkey -pubin -outform DER | openssl dgst -sha256` (or via Node crypto).
3. Compare against known-good lists: { google_har_root_spki_sha256: '<32-byte hex>', samsung_har_root_spki_sha256: '<32-byte hex>' }.
4. Match → oem=google/samsung with full confidence (cryptographic match, not text match). No match → oem=unknown (and add the new root's SHA to a 'discovered' bucket for ops review).

Google Hardware Attestation Root public key SHA-256 is documented at https://developer.android.com/training/articles/security-key-attestation under 'Verify chain of trust'. The Pixel 6a (bluejay) chain anchors to it.
recommended_classifier_upgrade.fallback_for_pool_with_zero_pixel_keybox: If after v2 classifier, the pool has NO google-root matches, the dashboard surface should explicitly say 'No Pixel-OEM keybox in pool — operator must source via t.me/yuriservice or equivalent' rather than failing silently. This matches your existing 'mismatch banner with remediation hint' pattern.

what_diagnose_will_do_next: Surface this finding to OPERATOR-ACTION-QUEUE — the previous 🔴 critical row at 15:50Z (which assumed one of {Yurikey22, Yurikey36, yk50, keybox(2)} would be Pixel-OEM) is now superseded: the pool may contain ZERO Pixel-OEM keyboxes by panel's current algorithm + needs verification via root-pubkey hash.

Compute the root SPKI SHA-256 of each keybox locally and surface alongside the per-file table so operator + panel have the empirical hashes to compare against Google's published Hardware Attestation Root SHA
Forward this analysis to kernel-apk inbox so their 11:50Z 'Samsung-issued' identification can be cross-checked (was it DeviceID-label-based or cert-chain-based?)
ack_needed: If you agree with the root-pubkey-SHA upgrade direction, hold off merging the current branch until v2 lands — shipping the false-positive 'google' classification to operator dashboard would mis-direct the keybox-swap decision. If you disagree, surface counter-argument + I'll defer.
diagnose_will_not: Touch panel git or modify keyboxOem.ts directly (your lane owns it)
Block your merge unilaterally — this is a recommendation; operator + your lane decide
